rss.channel:
Tre_brock https://Tre-brock.github.io/feed.xml I write about what I do, and what I like. https://notabug.org/Tre_brock/View-Github-Notifications-in-addon-bar
Tue, 14 May 2024 12:50:20 EDT A addon to view addons and edit preferences in xul based browsers
I love browsers like Seamonkey and Basilisk, but a problem I have is when you click the addons button, it just closes your tab and goes to about:addons. Modern Firefox lets you see your addons without leaving the current tab, and when you click the gear icon on the extention you can edit the preferences. So I made an addon to do this. When you click the extention (you might have to edit the toolbar and drag it there) it shows the about:addons page. You can disable and enable addons, remove them, or edit their preferences.
Wed, 8 May 2024 20:50:20 EDT How to add a Gmail account to Epyrus email client
https://tretechsolutions.com/img/gmailEpyrus.webm
Sun, 5 May 2024 01:31:20 EDT A slight redesign and upgrade for my website
I decided my website looked pretty boring. It was just grey background. So I made the background for things gradient and more colorful while still being dark. I also added a donation link on my addons page. By default the PayPal donation link tracks its users and reports to PayPal. So I removed the tracking pixel from my code and removed it needing to phone home to PayPal for the icon. It is now just stored on my website. The donation also included the first box on my website. It also is the first time I have put multiple things next to each other. I might start making my website less long and removing a lot of the unused space. Also on this website I changed the icon for the text "message me" to something that fit the background color more. I also moved the addons page higher because it is more important. On the contact page I added a signal icon that better contrast to the background. I also added the link to my IRC channel. Finally I made the images of Trisquel Gnu/Linux smaller because they are not as important as my actual work. That's it for my website cleanup.
Fri, 3 May 2024 18:31:20 EDT Seamonkey internet suite review The Seamonkey internet suite includes multiple programs in one. These are; browser, HTML editor, contact book, mail and RSS client, and a calendar. It uses the gecko rendering engine by Mozilla (also used by Firefox and Thunderbird). Seamonkey has a good ui, it has the different apps at the bottom of the browser and the bookmarks tabs and controls at the top. My one issue is that to close tabs you have to either right click a tab to close it or press the tab close button while you are on the tab you want to close. To fix this issue you can install. Seatab X 2 from the Seamonkey addon store. The Seamonkey browser is light on ram and is pretty fast. The mail client is smooth. I love how you can have many accounts at once and look through them all. Having your RSS feed with your mail is convenient too. Seamonkey also has a feature where it can detect RSS on a page. The HTML editor is good. It lets you resize images easily. You can edit the HTML page as if you are viewing it and not viewing code. It great to see it visually. But not being able to edit CSS is a bad spot, meaning you will need more then 1 editor. The IRC client of Seamonkey is actually the Firefox addons ChatZilla, and it works. Although it is an addon it feels like it was built for Seamonkey. The ui is basic but is fits Seamonkey. It works for being an irc client. It's speedy and clean. It also supports plugins itself and integrates into the Seamonkey settings. Finally I will be reviewing the calendar. It is built into the mail app, you get to it by clicking "Events and Tasks" at the top bar then clicking calendar. It's a pretty basic calendar app. It lets you use local calendars and use ones from the internet. It also lets you create tasks. It does what you think a calendar would do. It is also technically an addon called lightning. Overall Seamonkey is a great internet suite that I recommend you use. Ps: if you want a good ad and tracker blocker the look up ublock origin legacy and install that from GitHub.
Mon, 29 Apr 2024 20:50:10 EDT I made a new browser addon I made a new addon that lets you view github notifications and messages from cliking on the addon. Now you don't have to have a tab for checking on your messages. I don't use github that much anymore so it is nice not having to have it open for a notifaction here and there. The reason reaon this only supports Pale Moon and seamonkey is they support legacy addons format.
Mon, 29 Apr 2024 10:10:23 EDT My Pizza Tower Review Pizza Tower is one of the best games I have ever played. The music in this game is amazing, it is fast paced but does not feel life it is rushing you, and the sounds are also very good. The artwork in this game is amazing, at first it just looks like it was drawn in ms paint, but when you start playing you see that the animation is incredibly detailed and fluid. The art style is also amazing. But the best part of the game is the game play. There is so many different moves you can do in the game, but they all flow together perfectly. Almost every level does stop you from maintaining a super fast speed. If your reaction time is fast you can just keep mach running. Another part of the game is the ranks. They are from D-P (PSABCD). You can get a better rank by keeping your combo and getting secrets and the toppins. If you get all secrets all toppins and you keep you combo the whole time then you can get a P rank. Also there is a thing called lap 2. You play the level backwards a second time. You need to do this for a P rank. Recently the Devs who added a new playable character (he is also the third world boss). It is the noise. He looks simaler to the dominoes noid. The noise campaign is very different because his changed move set. Overall Pizza Tower is an amazing and repayable game that you should buy.
Sun, 28 Apr 2024 10:10:10 EDT I added RSS to my website I have watched a few videos on rss and read articles on and and have decided to add rss to my website, I left mastodon posting aout a month ago and havent posted on anything since. But now I will be posting on this rss feed!
